action.content:
You are playing a text adventure game. I will describe what you can perceive in the game. You write the single action you want to take in the game starting with >. Only reply with an action.
For example:
> open cupboard

You can have up to two objects in your inventory at the same time.

Your goal for this game is to brew a potion following the potion recipe.

Once you have achieved your goal, write "> done" to end the game.

You are in a bedroom now. There are a potion recipe, a pillow, a book, a wardrobe and a bed. The potion recipe is on the bed. The pillow is on the floor. The book is on the bed. The wardrobe is closed. There is a passage to a reading room here.
action.label: context

action.content: You take the potion recipe. In your inventory you have a potion recipe.
action.label: context

action.content:
You read the potion recipe:
That odd potion
Ingredients: Ectoplasm, spider, banana, green beetle.
1. Pour ectoplasm into your cauldron.
2. Put the spider into your cauldron.
3. Put the banana into your cauldron.
4. Use a fairy wand on your cauldron.
5. Wiggle a ice wand at your cauldron.
6. Add the green beetle into your cauldron.
action.label: context

action.content: > pour bucket of ectoplasm into cauldron
action.label: response

action.content:
You pour the contents of bucket of ectoplasm into the cauldron. The bucket of ectoplasm instantly refills itself.
The spider mingles with the ectoplasm with a puff of vapor producing a liquid in the cauldron.
The banana combines with the liquid in the cauldron with a gloopy sound.
The liquid in the glittery cauldron sloshes with a puff of vapor.
The liquid in the cold cauldron undulates with a gloopy sound.
The green beetle combines with the liquid with a phase shift leaving the finished potion in the cauldron.
action.label: context

action.content: Model produced DONE action at turn 70, end episode.
